name: Credit Literacy 101
description_short: Free financial literacy workshops hosted by the Free Library of Philadelphia in partnership with Citadel, offering credit and money management education to teens and adults.
description_long: Citadel Cares: Financial Literacy Program is a partnership between Citadel (a financial institution) and the Free Library of Philadelphia to provide free financial education workshops to residents. The program is hosted at the Northeast Regional Library in Philadelphia and targets teens and adults aged 15 and older. By leveraging a public library as the venue, the program makes financial education accessible to underserved communities without cost barriers. The workshops are instructor-led, interactive sessions designed to build practical financial competency. The program offers a structured four-workshop series covering essential money management topics: Credit 101 teaches credit fundamentals and building credit history; Understanding Debt covers loan and credit card management; Preparing for Financial Goals focuses on budgeting and long-term planning; and Managing Income and Expenses addresses spending tracking and financial health. Each workshop runs for one hour on Tuesday evenings, making them accessible to working adults and students. The program distinguishes itself by being genuinely free and embedded within a trusted public institution, removing accessibility barriers that paid financial coaching or for-profit credit counseling might present. Additionally, the partnership approach—combining a financial company's expertise with a library's community reach—creates a sustainable model for financial literacy education. The honest assessment is that while these workshops provide valuable foundational knowledge, they appear to be time-limited group sessions rather than ongoing one-on-one counseling, so participants with complex financial situations may need supplementary resources. The program's impact is also geographically limited to Philadelphia library locations and specific scheduled dates. Counselors can evaluate whether an installment loan for consolidation makes sense. For those with damaged credit, credit repair companies can dispute inaccurate items with all three bureaus. Secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to rebuilding credit scores over time.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Completely free workshops with no registration fees or hidden costs
Hosted at public library location, providing trusted, neutral environment
Age-appropriate programming for both teens (15+) and adults
Structured curriculum covering four essential financial topics across multiple sessions
Evening scheduling (5-6 p.m.) accommodates working adults and students
Interactive format emphasizes practical tools and skill-building rather than theory
Partnership with established financial institution (Citadel) provides expert instruction
cons: Limited to specific geographic location (Northeast Regional Library in Philadelphia only)
Fixed schedule with monthly sessions may not accommodate all schedules or learning paces
One-hour workshop format limits depth of coverage for complex financial topics
No indication of ongoing support, follow-up resources, or one-on-one counseling after workshops
Program appears to be time-limited (specific dates in 2026) with no indication of continuity beyond those dates
best_for: Philadelphia-area residents aged 15+ seeking foundational credit and money management knowledge
Teens and young adults building financial literacy before major life decisions
Working adults and students unable to afford paid financial coaching services
Community members preferring in-person group learning in trusted public spaces
diagnosis: This program is best for Philadelphia residents aged 15+ who need free, foundational financial education in a trusted public setting and have access to the Northeast Regional Library. The main caveat is that these are introductory group workshops rather than ongoing counseling or crisis intervention, so individuals with serious credit damage, active debt problems, or complex financial situations may need additional professional support beyond what these sessions provide.
services: Credit 101 workshop — understanding how credit works and building credit history
Understanding Debt workshop — strategies for managing loans and credit cards
Preparing for Financial Goals workshop — budgeting and long-term financial planning
Managing Income and Expenses workshop — spending tracking and financial health improvement
Interactive financial empowerment sessions for teens and adults
Practical tools for making confident financial decisions
Group-based financial education at public library locations
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 1401 E Bristol St Office #2, V23, Philadelphia, PA 19124
